mechanism_narrative: A higher federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and tightens financing conditions, causing lower S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when the level signal is elevated.
failure_condition: Reject if higher FEDFUNDS levels do not predict lower 20-day S&P 500 returns, measured by a nonnegative walk-forward slope or Rank IC above -0.03 in absolute failure over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A breakout in retail sales signals accelerating household demand and near-term revenue momentum for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days after the breakout.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered RSAFS breakouts are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of cases or if the mean triggered return is not above the unconditional mean at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor income and recession risk, reducing expected corporate earnings and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 4.0% or rises more than 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE-triggered observations produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if triggered returns are not below unconditional returns over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is high while unemployment is already elevated, restrictive policy coincides with weakening labor demand, increasing downside earnings risk and causing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 3.5% while UNRATE is above 4.0%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint FEDFUNDS-UNRATE trigger does not have lower 20-day returns than either single-series trigger alone, or if its interaction coefficient is not negative in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A stronger industrial production level signals improving real output capacity and cyclical earnings support, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher INDPRO levels or positive INDPRO trends fail to produce a positive 60-day return slope, or if walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 for two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts below a low activity threshold signals weakening construction demand and household confidence, reducing cyclical earnings expectations and causing negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-below-1300 triggers are followed by 60-day S&P 500 returns that are not below unconditional returns, or if the downside hit rate is below 55% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer prices are rising while the policy rate is already elevated, markets price a lower probability of near-term easing, pushing discount rates higher and producing negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth crosses above 3.0% while FEDFUNDS is above 3.0%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS trigger does not predict lower 5-day returns than CPIAUCSL alone, or if triggered 5-day average returns are nonnegative in more than half of rolling samples.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth combined with stable unemployment signals continued income formation without labor-market stress, supporting consumption and earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth crosses above 0 while UNRATE does not rise more than 0.1pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-positive and UNRATE-stable interaction fails to outperform the unconditional 20-day S&P 500 return, or if its interaction coefficient is not positive in walk-forward tests.
